Article Title: 'How dare you threaten students': Rahul Gandhi slams Delhi University over Jantar Mantar advisory
Article Text:
In its advisory, the university said demonstrations at Jantar Mantar are "strictly regulated" under Supreme Court directives and warned that participation in unlawful gatherings could invite legal action

NEW DELHI: Leader of opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi on Friday criticised the University of Delhi (DU) over its advisory asking students to stay away from demonstrations at Jantar Mantar. The Congress leader accused the university of threatening students for exercising their democratic rights.Reacting to the advisory, Gandhi wrote on X: "How dare you threaten students for exercising their democratic rights? Please note, you are the ones that will be held accountable when the time comes."The remarks came after DU issued a safety advisory urging students and faculty to avoid "unlawful assemblies or demonstrations" at Jantar Mantar amid ongoing protests over the alleged NEET-UG paper leak.In its advisory, the university said demonstrations at Jantar Mantar are "strictly regulated" under Supreme Court directives and warned that participation in unlawful gatherings could invite legal action. It also cautioned that such activities could pose risks to students' personal safety and adversely affect their academic progress and future professional opportunities."Accordingly, all students are urged to stay away from Jantar Mantar and to prioritise their safety and compliance with the law," the advisory said.DU also warned students against misinformation, saying that "a significant amount of fake and misleading content is being created and circulated to fuel the situation" and advised them to exercise caution before believing or sharing such material.Gandhi's criticism came a day after he, along with other INDIA bloc leaders, attempted to march to Gandhi Smriti in solidarity with students protesting against the alleged NEET-UG paper leak. The Opposition said the visit was also meant to pay tribute to students who allegedly died by suicide following the controversy and to express support for those injured during the Cockroach Janta Party (CJP)-led protests.Addressing reporters on Thursday, Rahul accused the Centre of "brutalising students" and demanded the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan.The protest saw tense scenes after Congress workers attempted to move past police barricades, leading to scuffles with Delhi Police personnel. Police warned that no sit-in protest or gathering was permitted at the site and said legal action would be taken if demonstrators failed to disperse.
Article Summary: Rahul Gandhi criticises the University of Delhi over its advisory asking students to stay away from demonstrations at Jantar Mantar. The Congress leader accused the university of threatening students for exercising their democratic rights. The remarks came after DU issued a safety advisory urging students and faculty to avoid "unlawful assemblies or demonstrations"

Article Title: SC issues notice to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla against approval of Sena (UBT) MPs' merger with Shinde faction
Article Text:
SC issues notice to Om Birla (ANI images)

Moved to SC earlier

Merger alters Lok Sabha numbers

NEW DELHI: The Supreme Court on Wednesday issued notices to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla, the Lok Sabha Secretary and six rebel Shiv Sena MPs on a plea filed by the Uddhav Balasaheb Thackeray (UBT) faction challenging the Speaker's decision to recognise the merger of the six MPs with the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena.The top court has further posted the matter for hearing after two weeks.The plea was filed by UBT MP Arvind Ganpat Sawant against the Speaker's decision, which was approved ahead of the Monsoon Session of Parliament. A bench comprising Justices PS Narasimha and Alok Aradhe issued notice to the respondents while refusing to stay the Speaker's order at this stage.The Shiv Sena (UBT) on Tuesday approached the Supreme Court challenging Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla's decision to approve the merger of its six MPs with the Shiv Sena led by Maharashtra deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde.Senior advocate Devadatt Kamat, appearing for the Uddhav Thackeray-led party, mentioned the matter before a bench comprising Chief Justice Surya Kant and Justices Joymalya Bagchi and Vipul M. Pancholi, seeking an urgent hearing on Wednesday.Ahead of the Monsoon Session, Speaker Om Birla approved the merger of six Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs with the Shinde-led Shiv Sena.The MPs are Sanjay Deshmukh (Yavatmal), Sanjay Jadhav (Parbhani), Sanjay Dina Patil (Mumbai North East), Nagesh Patil-Ashtikar (Hingoli), Omprakash Rajenimbalkar (Dharashiv) and Bhausaheb Wakchaure (Shirdi).With the merger, the Uddhav Thackeray-led Shiv Sena's strength in the Lok Sabha has come down to three MPs, while the Shinde-led Shiv Sena's tally has increased to 13 MPs, making it the third-largest constituent of the NDA after the BJP and the Telugu Desam Party (TDP).Reacting to the development, Maharashtra deputy chief minister Eknath Shinde said the six MPs joined his party to secure development for their constituencies and not for "personal gains".
Article Summary: Supreme Court issues notices to Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla, the Lok Sabha Secretary and six rebel Shiv Sena MPs. Uddhav Balasaheb Thackeray (UBT) faction challenging Speaker's decision to recognise the merger of the six MPs with the Eknath Shinde-led ShivSena.

Article Title: No Parliament business, four days into monsoon session
Article Text: NEW DELHI: If the first four days are any indication, monsoon session of Parliament is staring at a washout, with the opposition digging in its heels over its demand for resignation of education minister Dharmendra Pradhan, rebuffing invitations from govt for a discussion, reports Subodh Ghildiyal.Amid protests, Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha failed to transact business for the fourth consecutive day on Thursday. The stalemate could not be broken despite Speaker Om Birla meeting Rahul Gandhi, Akhilesh Yadav and Supriya Sule to defuse the situation. Home minister Amit Shah and parliamentary affairs minister Kiren Rijiju also met Birla.Opposition MPs took out a 'candlelight march' Thursday to the Gandhi Smriti in solidarity with the protesting students, two days after they held a dharna outside the PM's residence and were forcibly evacuated by police.The MPs belonging to Congress, SP, TMC, Left parties, RJD, Shiv Sena and others met at Rahul Gandhi's residence. From there, they marched to the Gandhi Smriti holding their phone torches. The marchers included Rahul, K C Venugopal, Ramjilal Suman, Supriya Sule, Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, N K Premachandran, John Brittas, Sandosh Kumar, Sudhakar Singh, Misa Bharti, Arvind Sawant, Saugata Roy and Mahua Moitra.Rahul said, "We go to remember the students we lost - the children driven to take their own lives after the NEET paper leak.And we go to stand with the students who lie injured today - beaten for peacefully demanding justice and accountability. India's students are not alone. The entire opposition stands with them and their demands."Congress's P Chidambaram said, "All roads are barricaded, and hundreds of policemen are mobilised to 'secure' the roads of Delhi. What is govt afraid of? Why this sense of panic?" Earlier, Congress rubbished PM Modi's statement assuring the protesting youth. Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge said the students don't need platitudes after govt used pellet guns, shock batons and tear gas on them.
Article Summary: Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha failed to transact business for the fourth consecutive day on Thursday. The stalemate could not be broken despite Speaker Om Birla meeting Rahul Gandhi, Akhilesh Yadav and Supriya Sule. Opposition MPs took out a 'candlelight march' Thursday to the Gandhi Smriti.

Article Title: Omar Abdullah to JP Nadda: Right on J&K leak, wrong on facts
Article Text: SRINAGAR: J&K chief minister Omar Abdullah rejected Thursday Union minister JP Nadda ’s claim linking “National Conference (NC) and Congress” to a govt job exam leak in the Union territory, pointing out that the fiasco occurred in 2022 under the lieutenant governor’s (LG) administration when his govt was not in place.Omar indicated that Nadda was correct about the Service Selection Board (SSB) exam leak but got other facts wrong. “It was 2022. Govt wasn’t of NC and Congress. It was a govt headed by LG but thank you for reminding everyone of this failure,” Omar posted.The CM’s response came a day after health minister Nadda accused Omar’s NC and ally Congress of maintaining silence on recruitment-related irregularities, mentioning the “SSB paper leak in J&K, where govt is of Congress and NC”.NC spokesperson and MLA Tanvir Sadiq seized on Nadda’s remarks to accuse “BJP of spreading falsehood” “Such blatant misinformation from a Union minister is both irresponsible and unbecoming,” Sadiq said.The tainted 2022 SSB exams, held for several posts like sub-inspectors and junior engineers, were cancelled over the leak. J&K high court ordered the administration to constitute a high-level probe panel.Posting the HC order, Omar mentioned the court’s observations. “We have no idea what happened to the high-level committee or its report. Perhaps you (Nadda) can share that with us in your next press conference,” Omar wrote.
Article Summary: J&K chief minister Omar Abdullah rejected Thursday Union minister JP Nadda ’s claim linking “National Conference (NC) and Congress’ to a govt job exam leak in the Union territory. “Such blatant misinformation from a Union minister is both irresponsible and unbecoming,” NC spokesperson and MLA Tanvir Sadiq said.

Article Title: Brother and sister attempt suicide at lodge in Maharashtra, both stable
Article Text: Brother and sister attempt suicide at lodge in Maharashtra, both stable
Article Summary: Brother and sister attempt suicide at lodge in Maharashtra, both stable. Brother and sister tried to kill themselves at a lodge in the state of Maharashtra. Both are in a stable condition, according to hospital officials. The incident happened at the lodge in Pune, Maharashtra, on Saturday.

Article Title: Maha forms high-level panel to probe alleged irregularities in Nizam-granted 4,120 acres of Tuljabhavani temple land
Article Text:
Among the allegations under scrutiny are claims that land was transferred to private individuals despite restrictions on its sale, allegedly in connivance with revenue officials

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:





Maharashtra govt has constituted a high-level four-member committee to investigate alleged irregularities involving 4,120 acres of land granted to the Tuljabhavani Temple Devasthan by the erstwhile Nizam of Hyderabad, directing the panel to submit its findings within a month.According to a govt resolution issued by the revenue and forest department on July 20, the committee will be chaired by the Pune divisional commissioner, with the collectors of Ahilyanagar and Solapur as members and the additional commissioner (revenue) in the Pune divisional commissioner’s office serving as member secretary.The panel has been tasked with independently examining allegations of illegal land transfers, manipulation of revenue records and loss of possession of temple land. It will verify revenue records, legal documents and other evidence before submitting a point-wise report to state govt.Among the allegations under scrutiny are claims that land was transferred to private individuals despite restrictions on its sale, allegedly in connivance with revenue officials. The committee will also examine assertions that while revenue records show 3,397 acres in the temple’s name, possession of a substantial portion has passed into private hands.The inquiry will further probe the alleged transfer of 403 acres belonging to the Jagdamba Trust, the alleged illegal mutation of 261 acres in favour of private individuals through misuse of authority by revenue officials, and disputed mutation entries involving 158 acres in Apsinga and Upla villages. The committee has been directed to verify all relevant records and mutation entries.The state-level probe comes weeks after Dharashiv collector Keerthi Kiran Pujar constituted a Special Investigation Team (SIT) to investigate complaints of large-scale encroachment of temple land. The three-member SIT was asked to examine revenue records, verify legal titles and probe encroachment allegations, though no deadline was fixed due to the complexity of the inquiry involving extensive Inam lands.The district-level investigation was initiated after the Rashtriya Mandir Mahasangh submitted a complaint to the revenue department on June 25 alleging large-scale encroachment of temple land. Revenue minister Chandrashekhar Bawankule subsequently directed the Dharashiv administration on June 29 to investigate the allegations.Sunil Ghanwat of the Rashtriya Mandir Mahasangh had alleged, citing govt records, that the erstwhile Nizam of Hyderabad had granted over 4,120 acres to the Tuljabhavani temple and that much of the land was allegedly lost over the years due to encroachments and irregular transactions involving officials and others.
Article Summary: Maharashtra govt has constituted a high-level four-member committee to investigate alleged irregularities involving 4,120 acres of land granted to the Tuljabhavani Temple Devasthan by the erstwhile Nizam of Hyderabad. The panel has been tasked with independently examining allegations of illegal land transfers, manipulation of revenue records and loss of possession of temple land.

Article Title: Jantar Mantar violence echoes in KDMC house meet as opposition, MNS protest police action
Article Text:
As soon as the proceedings began, opposition members stood up, shouted slogans and disrupted the House.





KALYAN: The Kalyan-Dombivli Municipal Corporation (KDMC) General Body meeting on Thursday witnessed high drama as opposition corporators staged a protest against the alleged police action against students demonstrating at Delhi's Jantar Mantar.Corporators from the opposition, including the Shiv Sena (UBT), Congress and NCP (SP), entered the House carrying the Tricolour and wearing black armbands as a mark of protest. Raising slogans of "Bharat Mata Ki Jai" and "Vande Mataram", they demanded that the House be adjourned to condemn the police action and express solidarity with the injured protesters.As soon as the proceedings began, opposition members stood up, shouted slogans and disrupted the House, forcing Mayor Harshali Chaudhary to adjourn the meeting for 10 minutes.However, after the House reconvened, the opposition continued its protest, condemning what it termed police brutality at Jantar Mantar and urging corporators from the ruling alliance to join them.The protest also witnessed an unusual political alignment, with the Maharashtra Navnirman Sena (MNS), a ruling ally of the Shiv Sena in the KDMC, joining hands with the Shiv Sena (UBT) during the demonstration. The BJP took a swipe at the MNS, saying that if MNS corporators wished to sit with the opposition, they should first resign from the ruling alliance and formally move to the opposition benches.Differences within the ruling alliance also came to the fore during the debate. Shiv Sena corporator Mahesh Gaikwad demanded that the House observe a two-minute silence in memory of the students who were allegedly injured in the police action. BJP group leader Shashikant Kamble objected, stating that several police personnel were also injured during the Jantar Mantar protest and that, if a condolence gesture was to be made, it should include them as well.Despite the noisy scenes and repeated slogan-shouting, the General Body meeting continued. Later, leaders of the Shiv Sena and BJP jointly cleared several civic proposals without detailed discussion, while a few agenda items were deferred for consideration at a later meeting.
Article Summary: Corporators from the opposition, including the Shiv Sena (UBT), Congress and NCP (SP), entered the House carrying the Tricolour and wearing black armbands as a mark of protest. Raising slogans of "Bharat Mata Ki Jai" and "Vande Mataram", they demanded that the House be adjourned.

Article Title: Protests in Nashik, Dhule, Jalgaon seeking Pradhan’s resignation
Article Text:
MNS workers protest against the police lathicharge on agitating students in Delhi

Nashik

:





The ongoing protests in Delhi demanding Union education minister Dharmendra Pradhan 's resignation over the NEET paper leak issue echoed across north Maharashtra on Wednesday, with Congress and Maharashtra Navnirman Sena (MNS) workers staging demonstrations in several districts, while BJP launched a counter-protest in Nashik.In Nashik, Congress workers and MNS activists protested against the lathicharge on students demonstrating at Jantar Mantar. BJP, however, accused opposition parties of hijacking the agitation and held a protest outside the Congress office.MNS functionaries staged a demonstration at the district collectorate and also attempted a rasta roko to condemn the police action against students in Delhi. State vice-president Salim Shaikh, Ratankumar Icham, Ankush Pawar and Sudam Kombde were among those who participated in the agitation.Bharatiya Janata Yuva Morcha (BJYM) protested outside the Congress office, alleging that anti-national and anti-Hindu slogans had been raised during the Delhi demonstrations.BJP Nashik president Sunil Kedar launched a sharp attack on the agitation and the opposition parties backing it. “Cockroach Janta Party, born out of social media abroad, has started an agitation over the paper leak. During this protest, anti-RSS and anti-national slogans were raised.Opposition parties, including Congress and the Samajwadi Party, jumped into this agitation, causing it to deviate from its core issue. Congress and other parties provoked youths who had no connection to this movement to escalate the agitation,” Kedar alleged.Congress workers, led by city president Nilesh Khaire, staged protests at the district collectorate and outside the party office in Nashik, raising slogans against the Delhi Police and BJP over the handling of the student agitation. The police deployed heavy bandobast across the city to prevent any untoward incidents.Similar protests were organised by Congress in Malegaon, Jalgaon and Dhule, while MNS activists also staged demonstrations in Dhule in support of the students' movement.
Article Summary: Congress and Maharashtra Navnirman Sena (MNS) workers staged demonstrations in several districts, while BJP launched a counter-protest in Nashik. MNS functionaries staged a demonstration at the district collectorate and also attempted a rasta roko to condemn the police action against students in Delhi.

Article Title: Man trampled to death by elephant near Dharmapuri
Article Text:
Representative image





DHARMAPURI: A 36-year-old man, who had been missing for three days, was found trampled to death by a wild elephant near the Kalappampadi forest area in Dharmapuri district on Wednesday.The deceased has been identified as Subramani of Athimarathur village near Pennagaram. Subramani, son of Chinnasamy and his wife Sivabakkiam, was suffering from mental illness.He went missing on Sunday, and family members and relatives searched for him. On Wednesday, they found his dead body near a farm well. Villagers informed forest and police officials, who reached the spot, recovered the body and sent it to Dharmapuri Govt Medical College and Hospital for postmortem. Investigations revealed that he had been trampled to death by an elephant, police said.
Article Summary: The deceased has been identified as Subramani of Athimarathur village near Pennagaram. He went missing on Sunday, and family members and relatives searched for him. On Wednesday, they found his dead body near a farm well. Investigations revealed he had been trampled to death by an elephant.

Article Title: Man dies after being attacked by wife near Coimbatore
Article Text: COIMBATORE: A 53-year-old vegetable seller died in Coimbatore Medical College Hospital on Monday night after he was assaulted by his wife during a drunken quarrel near Sirumugai in Coimbatore district.The deceased has been identified as K Chenniappan, 53, of Periyur in Palathottam. He was selling vegetables on a pushcart. His wife, Pushpalatha, 47, also sold vegetables using a pushcart. The couple have two sons.According to police sources, on July 17 night, Chenniappan, who was in an inebriated state, went to his wife’s vegetable pushcart, picked up a fight with her and assaulted her. Enraged, Pushpalatha picked up a log from the ground and struck him on the head.Chenniappan, who sustained severe head injuries, was rushed to Mettupalayam govt hospital. Later, he transferred to Coimbatore Medical College Hospital for further treatment. However, he died on Monday night without responding to treatment.The Sirumugai police registered a case and arrested Pushpalatha.
Article Summary: The deceased has been identified as K Chenniappan, 53, of Periyur in Palathottam. He was selling vegetables on a pushcart. His wife, Pushpalatha, 47, also sold vegetables using a push cart. The couple have two sons.

Article Title: Sengottaiyan flays previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies on Mekedatu dam issue
Article Text:
K A Sengottaiyan





ERODE: Tamil Nadu revenue minister K A Sengottaiyan on Saturday criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu.Speaking at a consultation meeting of officials from different departments held at the Erode district collectorate, Sengottaiyan reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move to build a dam in Mekedatu.During the review meeting, the minister told officials to deliver results quickly, saying the govt’s performance since coming to power warranted a thorough review and that the people’s demands should be met without delay. “Officers must complete necessary office procedures promptly and ensure citizens receive timely relief,” he told reporters after the meeting.The review, chaired by district collector S Kandasamy and also attended by handloom minister M Vijay Balaji, covered a wide range of administrative concerns.A major focus was the Athikadavu-Avinashi integrated drinking water project. Officials were directed to find out why more than 70 ponds were not getting filled and asked them to take remedial measures. Officials were instructed to begin silt-removal operations in the Bhavanisagar Dam to improve storage capacity and downstream supply.On allegations that liquor outlets were operating in violation of regulations, the revenue minister demanded concrete statistics and warned that strict action would follow where breaches were confirmed.
Article Summary: K A Sengottaiyan criticized the previous DMK govt for not having pursued legal remedies to stop Karnataka’s move to build a dam across the Cauvery river in Mekedatu. He reiterated that chief minister C Joseph Vijay had urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to halt the move.

Article Title: HC seeks reports from state on 2018 Tuticorin police firing incident
Article Text: Madras high court on Thursday directed the state to file reports on various aspects including the relief granted to the family members of the victims who died in the police firing on anti-Sterlite protestors in Tuticorin district in 2018.
Article Summary: Madras high court on Thursday directed the state to file reports on various aspects including the relief granted to the family members of the victims who died in the police firing on anti-Sterlite protestors in Tuticorin district in 2018.

Article Title: HC dismisses anticipatory bail plea of accused in Palani mutt land scam case
Article Text: Madras high court on Wednesday dismissed the anticipatory bail plea of K Vellathurai of Tirupur
Article Summary: Madras high court dismisses anticipatory bail plea of K Vellathurai of Tirupur. Vellithurai is accused of raping a woman in a hotel room in Chennai. He is also accused of assaulting another woman in the same hotel room.

Article Title: 15 injured in fireworks blast during church festival in Dindigul
Article Text:
As many as 15 people sustained injuries while crackers were burst

Madurai:





As many as 15 people sustained injuries while crackers were burst during the annual festival of St Mary Magdalene Church in Vakkampatti, Dindigul district, on Tuesday midnight.The festival commenced with the flag-hoisting ceremony on July 18. The holy car procession is one of the important events of the festival, which was held on Tuesday midnight. Hundreds of people gathered to witness the holy car festival.According to sources, aerial crackers were burst in front of the church before the procession commenced. As the crackers were being burst, a spark fell on a huge quantity of crackers stacked a few metres away from the church, which triggered a series of explosions. In this incident, 15 people sustained injuries. They were immediately rushed to the Dindigul Govt Medical College Hospital for treatment.Subsequently, Barath Krishna, 20, of Kalnattanpatti, who sustained severe injuries, was referred to the Govt Rajaji Hospital in Madurai for treatment. Similarly, Karan, 24, of Kalnattanpatti, who sustained severe injuries, was admitted to a private hospital in Dindigul. The Dindigul Taluk police registered a case and are conducting investigations.
Article Summary: As many as 15 people sustained injuries while crackers were burst during the annual festival of St Mary Magdalene Church in Vakkampatti, Dindigul district. The holy car procession is one of the important events of the festival, which was held on Tuesday midnight.

Article Title: Court denies bail to MLA Markandayan
Article Text:
Denied bail to Vilathikulam MLA G V Markandayan,

Tuticorin:





A Tuticorin court on Wednesday denied bail to Vilathikulam MLA G V Markandayan, who was arrested for allegedly making inflammatory remarks against chief minister C Joseph Vijay.Markandayan was booked under three sections after a complaint alleged that he made threatening remarks against the chief minister during a public meeting in Kovilpatti on July 18. He was remanded in judicial custody and lodged in Palayamkottai Central Prison on Monday.The MLA moved a bail petition before Judicial Magistrate-I, Tuticorin, on Wednesday, while police sought an extension of his judicial custody.The court rejected both the bail petition and the police’s request to extend his judicial custody.Speaking to party cadres from the police vehicle, Markandayan said, “The prison conditions are comfortable, and I am happy. There is no need to file a bail petition again,” adding that enduring hardship would help the DMK return to power sooner.
Article Summary: Vilathikulam MLA G V Markandayan was arrested for allegedly making inflammatory remarks against chief minister C Joseph Vijay. He was booked under three sections after a complaint alleged that he made threatening remarks against the chief minister during a public meeting in Kovilpatti on July 18.

Article Title: Kerala HC upholds magistrate court’s order dismissing discharge pleas of Muttil tree-felling accused
Article Text:
According to prosecution, the petitioners cheated M M Aliyar of Karimukal in Ernakulam by selling him timber allegedly felled and transported illegally from the area under the Meppadi forest range

Kochi:





High court has upheld the Chottanikkara magistrate court’s decision to proceed with the case against Roji Augustine, Jose Kutty Augustine and Anto Augustine in a cheating case arising out of the alleged illegal felling and transportation of trees from South Muttil in the Meppadi forest range of Wayanad district.Justice G Girish dismissed the petitions filed by the three accused challenging the Chottanikkara judicial first-class magistrate court’s order refusing to quash the case registered against them at the Ambalamedu police station in Ernakulam. HC further observed that there were sufficient grounds to proceed against the petitioners for the offence punishable under Section 420 read with Section 34 of IPC.According to prosecution, the petitioners cheated M M Aliyar of Karimukal in Ernakulam by selling him timber allegedly felled and transported illegally from the area under the Meppadi forest range. Aliyar was allegedly induced to believe that the timber was accompanied by the requisite passes issued by forest department. On finding that the allegations disclosed a prima facie case, the magistrate court dismissed the petition seeking to quash the proceedings.Before HC, the petitioners contended that Aliyar’s complaint was wholly unsustainable as a Form III pass was not required for transporting the timber sold to him. However, HC rejected the contention, noting that the forest department had already initiated proceedings against the petitioners for allegedly felling and removing trees in violation of the relevant provisions of law. In such circumstances, HC held that a prima facie case of cheating had been made out and declined to interfere with the magistrate court’s order.
Article Summary: Roji Augustine, Jose Kutty Augustine and Anto Augustine are accused of cheating. The case relates to alleged illegal felling and transportation of trees from South Muttil in the Meppadi forest range of Wayanad district. According to prosecution, the petitioners cheated M M Aliyar of Karimukal in Ernakulam.

Article Title: Repeat offender arrested for mobile phone theft at Kizhakkekotta
Article Text:
Kochi

:





A 39-year-old man accused of stealing numerous mobile phones was arrested by Hill Palace police. The accused, Jijo Babu of Malayattoor, allegedly targeted women passengers by stealing mobile phones from crowded buses and bus stops across city.According to police, Babu is an accused in multiple theft cases registered at various police stations in Ernakulam. Investigators suspect he may have been involved in more than 100 mobile phone thefts and alleged that stolen phones were sold with the help of accomplices.Babu was caught from Kizhakkekotta bus stop by senior CPO Vinod Vasudevan on Wednesday.Arrest followed a complaint lodged by a 58-year-old woman from Amballoor, who alleged that her mobile phone was snatched while she was boarding a bus at Kizhakkekotta bus stop in Tripunithura on Tuesday evening.Complainant told police that an unidentified person forcibly snatched her mobile phone, valued at Rs 20,000, from the pocket of her coat as she was getting onto the bus.Hill Palace police registered a case under Section 304(2) of Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) and launched an investigation. Babu was produced before a court and remanded in judicial custody.
Article Summary: Jijo Babu, 39, of Malayattoor, allegedly targeted women passengers by stealing mobile phones from crowded buses and bus stops across city. Investigators suspect he may have been involved in more than 100 mobile phone thefts and alleged that stolen phones were sold with the help of accomplices.
